subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
In general from left to right in a period, ionisation enthalpy increases due to effective nuclear charge increases. but due to extra stability of half filled and full filled electronic configuration, required ionisation enthalpy is more from neighbouring elements.
i.e. first ionisation enthalpy order is

Al<Mg<Si<S<P

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
On the periodic table, electronegativity generally increases as you move from left to right across a period and decreases as you move down a group. As a result, the most electronegative elements are found on the top right of the periodic table, while the least electronegative elements are found on the bottom left.

Element             Electronegativity

P                         2.1

C                           2.5

Br                          3.0                   

At                           2.2

Hence, the correct order is Br > C > At > P.

chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mfenced></math> are correct and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mfenced></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math>

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mfenced></math> are correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mfenced></math> is not the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math>.
solution: It is due to increase in ionisation enthalpy and increases in electron gain enthalpy, when one moves from left to right in a period.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
The six commonly recognised metalloids are boron, silicon, germanium, arsenic, antimony, and tellurium.

Hence. tellurium is a metalloid.

All other options are metals.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
In a period, as we move from left to right, the atomic size decreases and the effective nuclear charge increases.

Due to the large atomic size and low effective nuclear charge, the element at the extreme left (alkali metal) has the lowest first ionisation enthalpy in the period.

As we move to the extreme right (excluding noble gases), the elements (halogens) have the smallest atomic size and highest effective nuclear charge in the period. They require only one electron to complete their octet. Thus, they have the highest negative electron gain enthalpy.

Therefore, the first ionisation enthalpy of the extreme left element is the lowest, and the negative electron gain enthalpy of the extreme right element is the highest.

Answer: lowest and highest

chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

Al3+<Mg2+<Na+<F-<O2-<N3-

All are isoelectronic species so more is the z of less will be the ionic size.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
The basicity of an oxide is closely related to the electropositive character of the element. The more electropositive the element, the more basic its oxide will be. This is because the oxide ion can react with a cation to form a basic oxide. As we move down Group 13, the electropositive character of the elements increases, and so does the basicity of their oxides.

B2O3<Al2O3<Ga2O3<In2O3<Tl2O3

So most basic oxide among the given options is Tl2O3.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
As Z e ↑ ionic radius decreases for isoelectronic species.

Z=number of protonse=number of electrons

N 3 - Z e = 7 1 0

O 2 - Z e = 8 1 0

F - Z e = 9 1 0

Trend of ionic radius: N 3 - > O 2 - > F -

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: Single $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{N}$ bond is weaker than the single $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{P}$ bond,
The stability of hydrides increases from $\mathrm{NH}_3$ to $\mathrm{BiH}_3$ in group 15 of the periodic table
solution: Stability of hydrides decreases down the group from $\mathrm{NH}_3$ to $\mathrm{BiH}_3$ as $\mathrm{M}-\mathrm{H}$ bond energy decreases.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$ is the most acidic oxide among the dioxides of group of 14 elements.
Carbon exhibits negative oxidation states along with +4 and +2.
Carbon cannot exceed its covalency more than four.
Among the isotopes of carbon, ${ }^{13} \mathrm{C}$ is a radioactive isotope.
solution:
Analyzing each statement:
1 CO₂ is most acidic among group 14 dioxides - correct, it's a non-metal acidic oxide.
2 Carbon shows negative oxidation states (-4 in methane, -3 in ethane, etc.) along with +2 and +4 - correct.
3 Carbon covalency cannot exceed 4 - correct, as it's period 2 element unable to expand octet.
4 ¹³C is radioactive - incorrect. ¹³C is a stable, non-radioactive isotope. ¹⁴C is the radioactive isotope used in radiocarbon dating with half-life of 5730 years. Therefore, statement (4) is incorrect.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
Electron gain enthalpy values of the elements are given below in kJ/mol,

FCl Te  Po −333−349−190−174.

Down the group electron affinity decreases. The size of fluorine atom is small. The addition of electron to small fluorine atom results in electron-electron repulsions. Hence, fluorine has less electron affinity than chlorine.

Hence, the correct order is 

Cl>F>Te>PO

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
A metal is a material that, when freshly prepared, polished, or fractured, shows a lustrous appearance, and conducts electricity and heat relatively well. Metals are typically malleable (they can be hammered into thin sheets) or ductile (can be drawn into wires)

On moving top to bottom of the periodic table metallic character increases and on moving left to right metallic character decreases.

Metallic character is directly proportional to the atomic size.

The correct order of atomic size of the given elements is Na>Mg>Be>Si>P.

Hence, correct order of metallic character is Na>Mg>Be>Si>P

All these elements are form 3rd period except beryllium which is from second period

Na is an alkali metal

Mg and Be are alkaline earth metals, 

Phosphorous belongs to a group which is commonly called as pnicogens.

Si belongs to 14th group

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
Group 15 elements have electronegativity values (Pauling scale): N = 3.04, P = 2.19, As = 2.18, Sb = 2.05, Bi = 2.02.
The condition requires: |EN(X) - EN(P)| > |EN(P) - EN(Y)|.
(1) N and As: |3.04 - 2.19| = 0.85 and |2.19 - 2.18| = 0.01. Since 0.85 > 0.01, the condition is satisfied.
(2) As and Sb: |2.18 - 2.19| = 0.01 and |2.19 - 2.05| = 0.14. Since 0.01 < 0.14, it fails.
(3) Bi and N: |2.02 - 2.19| = 0.17 and |2.19 - 3.04| = 0.85. Since 0.17 < 0.85, it fails.
(4) As and Bi: |2.18 - 2.19| = 0.01 and |2.19 - 2.02| = 0.17. Since 0.01 < 0.17, it fails.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:

$E B>E A>E C>E D$

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
solution:
Ionization enthalpy is defined as the minimum amount of energy that is required to remove the most loosely bounded electron that is the electron present in the outermost shell, from an isolated gaseous atom.

First, ionisation energy increases along the period because effective nuclear charge increases. Along the period Z increases which outweighs the shielding effect.

Hence, option C is the answer.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I:
$Al_2O_3$ and $Cr_2O_3$ are both amphoteric oxides.
$Cl_2O_7$ and $Mn_2O_7$ are both acidic oxides.
$Na_2O$ and $V_2O_3$ are both basic oxides.
$CO$ and $N_2O$ are both neutral oxides.
All $4$ pairs contain oxides of the same nature. Hence, Statement I is true.

Statement II:
$Na_2O$ is a strongly basic oxide.
$Al_2O_3$ is an amphoteric oxide.
$CO$ is a neutral oxide.
$Cl_2O_7$ is a strongly acidic oxide.
Thus, the most basic oxide is $Na_2O$ and the most acidic oxide is $Cl_2O_7$. Hence, Statement II is true.

Both Statement I and Statement II are true.

Answer: Both Statement I and Statement II are true

chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution: Graphite is conductor

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
The expected order of melting points of group 13 elements is B>Al>Ga>In>Tl.

But due to structural changes, melting point instead of decreasing, increases from Ga to Tl and Ga has the lowest melting point. Hence, the actual order is B>Al>Tl>In>Ga

Melting point

Al→933 K

Ga→303 K

In→430 K

Se→490 K

Ga has low meting point and it is closer to metalloid in periodic table.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution: Follow the periodic trends

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
The ionisation enthalpy increases as we go from left to right in a period, while it decreases as we come down a group. So according to this, the order will be Be<B<C<N<O. But there are two exceptions, in the case of Be and N because of the highly stable 2s2 (completely filled orbital) and 2s22p3 (half-filled orbital 2p3) valence configuration. Due to this, the correct order is:
B<Be<C<O<N.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
solution:
In Cl2 molecule, the covalent radius is half of the internuclear distance, so statement I is false. The covalent radius is little smaller than the actual radius of the atom of chlorine due to overlapping during formation of covalent bond.

For the same element, anion has lower effective nuclear charge than atom so, anion is larger than the parent atom. That means statement II is correct.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: For group <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> elements, the stability of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn></math> oxidation state increases down the group.

Elements of group <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn></math> have lower ionization enthalpy values compared to those of group <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> in the corresponding periods.
The group <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>13</mn></math> elements are all metals.
All the elements in group <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>17</mn></math> are gases.
solution: Group 16 elements have less ionization potential than Group 15 elements due to half-filled configurations of group 15 elements. So need more amount of energy to remove an electron. In the halogen family iodine is solid and bromine is liquid. In the boron family boron is non metal.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution: Catenation is the tendency to link with one another through covalent bonds to form chain and rings, i.e., catenation. This is because bonds are very strong in the carbon family. On moving down the group, the size increases and the tendency to show catenation decreases because bond enthalpy decreases. The order of catenation is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>></mo><mi>Si</mi><mo>></mo><mi>Ge</mi><mo>></mo><mi>Pb</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Pb</mi></math> does not show catenation.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I: The second ionization enthalpy of Ga is higher than that of Al. This is due to the poor shielding effect of the ten $3d$ electrons in Ga, which increases the effective nuclear charge and holds the valence electrons more tightly. The correct order of second ionization enthalpy is $B > Ga > Al$. Thus, Statement I is false.

Statement II: The first ionization enthalpy generally decreases down the group. However, Pb has a slightly higher ionization enthalpy than Sn due to the poor shielding of $4f$ and $5d$ electrons (lanthanoid contraction). The correct order of first ionization enthalpy for Group 14 elements is $Sn < Pb < Ge < Si < C$. Thus, Statement II is false.

Both Statement I and Statement II are false.

Answer: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
